walkthrough.title: Unfold the Islands Board
walkthrough.text: Let's get Dice Forge set up. First, take out the Islands board and unfold it in the center of the table. This is the big board showing different portals where heroes can stand. Each portal is connected to a stack of Heroic Feat cards that you can earn during the game. Make sure everyone can reach the board comfortably.

walkthrough.title: Place the Temple on the Foundations
walkthrough.text: Now find the Temple board, which is the large vertical display that holds all the die faces you can buy. Place it on the two foundation stands so it sits upright on the table near the Islands board. The Temple has two areas: the Sanctuary on the left with ten pools of die faces that cost Gold, and the Gardens on the right with five pools of die faces that cost Sun or Moon Shards.

walkthrough.title: Stock the Temple with Die Faces
walkthrough.text: Sort all the removable die faces by type and place them in their matching pools on the Temple. Each pool has a specific spot with an icon showing which face goes where. The Sanctuary pools hold faces you buy with Gold, and the Gardens pools hold faces you buy with Sun Shards or Moon Shards. Stack multiples of the same face in their pool. Make sure all pools are filled before you start.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Heroic Feat Cards
walkthrough.text: Find the Heroic Feat cards. These are the larger cards with artwork showing different mythological feats. Sort them by type and place them in face-up stacks of four cards each on the matching portals of the Islands board. Each portal space shows which stack goes there. There are cards that cost Sun Shards, cards that cost Moon Shards, and cards that cost a combination of both.

walkthrough.title: Place the Round Tracker
walkthrough.text: Find the round tracker token and place it on the first space of the round track printed on the Islands board. For a three-player game you will play ten rounds. For a four-player game it is nine rounds, and for two players it is also nine rounds but with a special rule we will cover later. The round tracker moves forward one space at the end of each round.

walkthrough.title: Give Each Player a Hero Inventory
walkthrough.text: Hand each player one Hero Inventory board. This is the personal board with four resource tracks printed on it: Gold across the top going from zero to twelve, Sun Shards from zero to six, Moon Shards from zero to six, and Glory Points from zero all the way up to ninety-nine with a loop. Each track has a sliding marker that you will move up and down as you gain and spend resources.

walkthrough.title: Set Resource Markers to Zero
walkthrough.text: Give each player five resource markers in their color. Place one marker on the zero space of each of the four resource tracks on the Hero Inventory: Gold at zero, Sun Shards at zero, Moon Shards at zero, and Glory Points at zero. The fifth marker is a spare. Make sure all markers are clearly on zero before continuing.

walkthrough.title: Distribute Dice to Each Player
walkthrough.text: Each player gets two dice: one light-colored die and one dark-colored die. These are special dice with removable faces that snap on and off. Right now each die starts with basic faces: mostly one-Gold faces and a couple of other starting faces. Take a moment to examine your dice and notice how the faces can be pried off. You will be swapping these faces throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Give Each Player a Hero Pawn
walkthrough.text: Each player takes a hero pawn in their chosen color and places it on the starting portal on the Islands board. All players begin at the same portal. Your pawn will move to different portals when you perform Heroic Feats during the game.

walkthrough.title: Determine the First Player
walkthrough.text: The youngest player goes first. Give them whatever you like as a reminder, or just remember the turn order. Play proceeds clockwise from the first player. Turn order matters because it determines starting Gold.

walkthrough.title: Adjust Starting Gold by Turn Order
walkthrough.text: Here is an important balancing step. The first player starts with three Gold, the second player starts with two Gold, the third player starts with one Gold, and the fourth player starts with zero Gold. Move each player's Gold marker on their Hero Inventory to the correct starting value. This compensates for the advantage of going first, since earlier players get more turns overall.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the 100 Glory Point Tokens
walkthrough.text: Place the 100 Glory Point tokens in a pile near the board where everyone can reach them. If any player's Glory Points pass ninety-nine on their track, they take one of these tokens and reset their marker back to zero. Each token is worth one hundred points at the end of the game. That is the full setup. Let's learn how to play.

walkthrough.title: Round Structure Overview
walkthrough.text: Dice Forge is played over nine or ten rounds depending on player count. Each round, every player takes one turn as the active player. But here is what makes Dice Forge special: at the start of every single turn, ALL players roll their dice and collect resources, not just the active player. So you are always gaining something, even when it is not your turn. Let's walk through the four steps of each turn.

walkthrough.title: Step 1 — Divine Blessing
walkthrough.text: The very first thing that happens on every turn is the Divine Blessing. ALL players, not just the active player, pick up both of their dice and roll them. Whatever resources the dice show, you gain them immediately. Move your markers up on your Hero Inventory. If a die face shows two Gold, move your Gold marker up two. If it shows a Sun Shard, move that marker up one. Resources are capped at their maximum: Gold at twelve, Sun Shards at six, Moon Shards at six. Any excess is lost, so try to spend before you overflow. In a two-player game, each player rolls twice for their Divine Blessing instead of once.

walkthrough.title: Minor Blessing
walkthrough.text: Some card effects and game situations grant a Minor Blessing instead of a full Divine Blessing. A Minor Blessing means you roll only one of your two dice, and you choose which one to roll. This is important because your two dice often have different faces forged onto them, so picking the right die matters. Minor Blessings come from certain Heroic Feat card effects and from being ousted, which we will cover shortly.

walkthrough.title: Step 2 — Call for Reinforcements
walkthrough.text: After the Divine Blessing, only the active player may Call for Reinforcements. This means activating any reinforcement effects on Heroic Feat cards you have previously collected. Reinforcement cards have a special icon showing they can be activated each turn. You may activate your reinforcement effects in any order you choose, and each one can only be used once per turn. If you have no reinforcement cards, simply skip this step.

walkthrough.title: Step 3, Option A — Make an Offering to the Gods
walkthrough.text: Now the active player performs ONE action. Your first option is to Make an Offering to the Gods. This means buying new die faces from the Temple. Go to the Sanctuary or Gardens and pick a die face you want. Pay its Gold cost from your Hero Inventory by moving your Gold marker down, or pay its Sun or Moon Shard cost for Gardens faces. Then immediately forge the new face onto one of your dice. To forge, use the new face as a lever to pry off the old face you want to replace, then snap the new face into that slot. Keep the old face nearby in case you need to reference it. You may buy multiple faces in one Offering as long as you can afford them, but you cannot buy two copies of the same face in a single Offering.

walkthrough.title: Step 3, Option B — Perform a Heroic Feat
walkthrough.text: Your other action option is to Perform a Heroic Feat. Choose a Heroic Feat card from one of the portals on the Islands board. Pay its cost in Sun Shards, Moon Shards, or both by moving your markers down. Then move your hero pawn to that card's portal and take the top card from the stack. Some cards have instant effects that trigger right away. Some have reinforcement effects you can use on future turns. Some have passive effects that are always active. And most cards are worth Glory Points at the end of the game, shown in the corner.

walkthrough.title: Ousting Other Players
walkthrough.text: When you move your hero pawn to a portal that is already occupied by another player's pawn, you oust them. The ousted player's pawn is sent back to the starting portal, and as compensation they immediately receive a Minor Blessing, rolling one die of their choice and gaining those resources. Ousting is not always a bad thing for the other player since they get a free roll, but it does move them away from their current position.

walkthrough.title: Step 4 — Extra Action
walkthrough.text: After completing your main action, you may spend two Sun Shards to take one additional action. This extra action can be either Make an Offering or Perform a Heroic Feat, same as before. You can only do this once per turn, so you get at most two actions total. This is a powerful option when you have Sun Shards to spare and want to both upgrade your dice and grab a card in the same turn.

walkthrough.title: Card Effect Types
walkthrough.text: Heroic Feat cards come in three varieties of lasting effects. Instant effects happen once when you take the card and never again. Reinforcement effects can be activated once per turn during your Call for Reinforcements step. And passive effects are always active without needing activation. Cards are organized into three piles by their permanent effect type so you can easily scan what you have. Some cards have a combination, like an instant effect plus Glory Points but no ongoing power.

walkthrough.title: End of Round and Advancing the Tracker
walkthrough.text: Once every player has taken one turn as the active player, the round ends. Move the round tracker forward one space. Then begin the next round with the next player in clockwise order becoming the active player first. Continue until all rounds are complete.

walkthrough.title: Game End and Scoring
walkthrough.text: After the final round, the game is over. Each player totals their Glory Points. Add up your Glory Points track value, plus any 100 GP tokens you earned, plus the Glory Points printed on all Heroic Feat cards you collected. The player with the most Glory Points wins. In case of a tie, the player with the most Gold, Sun Shards, and Moon Shards remaining combined wins the tiebreaker.

walkthrough.title: Meet Our Three Heroes
walkthrough.text: Let's walk through the first round of a three-player game to see how everything fits together. Our players are Alex, who goes first with three starting Gold, Blake, who goes second with two Gold, and Casey, who goes third with one Gold. All three have their hero pawns on the starting portal, their Hero Inventories set up, and their two starting dice ready to go. Round one of ten begins now.

walkthrough.title: Alex's Turn — Divine Blessing for Everyone
walkthrough.text: Alex is the active player first. Before Alex does anything else, ALL three players perform the Divine Blessing. Everyone picks up both dice and rolls them. Alex rolls a one-Gold face and a one-Gold face, gaining two Gold total. Blake rolls a one-Gold and a one-Gold, also gaining two Gold. Casey rolls a one-Gold and a Sun Shard, gaining one Gold and one Sun Shard. Everyone moves their Hero Inventory markers up accordingly.

walkthrough.title: Check Alex's Resources
walkthrough.text: After the Divine Blessing, let's check where Alex stands. Alex started with three Gold and just gained two more, so Alex now has five Gold. That is enough to buy some interesting die faces from the Sanctuary. Sun and Moon Shards are both still at zero, and Glory Points are at zero.

walkthrough.title: Alex Skips Reinforcements
walkthrough.text: Step two is Call for Reinforcements, but Alex has no Heroic Feat cards yet since this is the very first turn of the game. Nothing to activate. Alex moves on to the main action.

walkthrough.title: Alex Chooses to Make an Offering
walkthrough.text: Alex decides to Make an Offering to the Gods. Alex looks at the Temple and spots a three-Gold die face in the Sanctuary that costs three Gold. That would be a huge upgrade from the basic one-Gold faces. Alex pays three Gold, moving the Gold marker from five down to two, and takes the three-Gold face from the Temple.

walkthrough.title: Alex Forges the New Face
walkthrough.text: Now Alex must immediately forge the new face onto one of the dice. Alex picks the light die and chooses to replace a one-Gold face. Using the new three-Gold face as a lever, Alex pries off the old one-Gold face and snaps the new three-Gold face into that slot. Alex keeps the old face nearby. From now on, every time Alex rolls that die and lands on that face, it will produce three Gold instead of one. Nice upgrade.

walkthrough.title: Alex Considers the Extra Action
walkthrough.text: Step four: Alex could spend two Sun Shards for an extra action. But Alex has zero Sun Shards, so that is not possible this turn. Alex's turn is over. The active player rotates to Blake.

walkthrough.title: Blake's Turn — Divine Blessing Again
walkthrough.text: Blake is now the active player. Once again, ALL players roll both dice for the Divine Blessing. Alex rolls and gets the shiny new three-Gold face plus a one-Gold face, gaining four Gold. That upgraded face is already paying off. Blake rolls two one-Gold faces, gaining two Gold. Casey rolls one Gold and one Gold, gaining two Gold. Everyone updates their Hero Inventories.

walkthrough.title: Mistake — Casey Forgets to Collect
walkthrough.text: Wait. Casey got distracted and forgot to roll during the Divine Blessing. This is a very common mistake in Dice Forge, especially for new players. Remember, EVERY player rolls during EVERY Divine Blessing, not just the active player. Casey quickly rolls both dice and collects the resources. In a real game, make it a habit: when the active player says 'Divine Blessing,' everyone picks up their dice together.

walkthrough.title: Blake Skips Reinforcements
walkthrough.text: Like Alex, Blake has no reinforcement cards yet. Blake skips straight to the main action.

walkthrough.title: Blake Chooses a Heroic Feat
walkthrough.text: Blake decides to Perform a Heroic Feat instead of buying die faces. Blake has been eyeing a card that costs one Sun Shard. Blake checks the Hero Inventory and has exactly one Sun Shard from earlier rolls. Blake pays one Sun Shard, moving that marker from one down to zero.

walkthrough.title: Blake Moves and Takes the Card
walkthrough.text: Blake moves the hero pawn from the starting portal to the portal matching the chosen card. Blake takes the top card from that stack. This card has an instant effect that grants a Minor Blessing, plus it is worth two Glory Points at end of game. Blake immediately resolves the instant effect by rolling one die of choice and collecting those resources. Blake also moves the Glory Points marker up... wait, actually no. Glory Points on cards are only counted at game end, not added to the track during play. Blake leaves the GP marker alone and sets the card aside.

walkthrough.title: Blake's Turn Ends
walkthrough.text: Blake has zero Sun Shards remaining, so the extra action is not an option. Blake's turn is complete. The active player rotates to Casey.

walkthrough.title: Casey's Turn — Another Divine Blessing
walkthrough.text: Casey is now the active player. Everyone performs the Divine Blessing. All three players roll both dice and gain resources. This time everyone remembers to roll together. Casey gains one Gold and one Moon Shard from this roll. Alex and Blake gain their respective resources as well.

walkthrough.title: Casey Skips to the Action
walkthrough.text: Casey has no reinforcement cards, so that step is skipped. Casey decides to Make an Offering to the Gods and heads to the Temple to browse die faces.

walkthrough.title: Mistake — Casey Tries to Buy Two of the Same Face
walkthrough.text: Casey sees a two-Gold die face that costs two Gold and wants to buy two copies of it to upgrade both dice at once. However, this is not allowed. You cannot buy two copies of the same die face in a single Offering action. Casey can buy one two-Gold face now and could buy a second copy on a future turn, or as an extra action if they had the Sun Shards. Casey buys just one two-Gold face for now.

walkthrough.title: Casey Forges and Finishes
walkthrough.text: Casey takes the two-Gold face and forges it onto the dark die, replacing a one-Gold face. Pry off the old face with the new one, snap it in. Done. Casey does not have two Sun Shards for an extra action, so the turn ends.

walkthrough.title: End of Round One
walkthrough.text: All three players have now taken a turn as the active player. That completes round one. Move the round tracker forward one space. Notice how in just one round, everyone rolled their dice three times during Divine Blessings, collecting resources each time, and Alex already has an upgraded die face that will compound in value every single round.

walkthrough.title: Preview of Round Two — Ousting
walkthrough.text: Let's peek ahead to see one more important concept. In round two, suppose Alex wants to Perform a Heroic Feat and moves to the same portal where Blake's pawn is sitting. Alex ousts Blake. Blake's pawn gets sent back to the starting portal, and Blake receives a Minor Blessing as compensation, rolling one die of choice and gaining those resources. Blake is not thrilled about being moved, but that free roll is a nice consolation.

walkthrough.title: The Rhythm of the Game
walkthrough.text: You can already feel the rhythm of Dice Forge. Every turn starts with everyone rolling, which keeps all players engaged even when it is not their turn. The active player then decides: upgrade my dice to build a better engine, or spend shards on powerful cards for Glory Points? That tension between investing in your dice and cashing in on cards is the heart of the game.

walkthrough.title: Before You Play
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First — check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second — reference the Appendix for a quick guide to die faces, card types, and game terms.

And most importantly — ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

summary.title: Before You Play
bullets: Check General Tips for strategy basics
Reference the Appendix for die faces, cards, and terms
Ask your personal GM Guide — tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Upgrade Your Gold Faces Early
walkthrough.text: The single most impactful thing you can do in the first few rounds is upgrade your dice with better Gold faces. Think of it like compound interest. If you forge a three-Gold face in round one, you roll that face every single Divine Blessing for the rest of the game. Over ten rounds with three players, that is around thirty rolls per die. A face you upgrade in round one pays dividends for the entire game, while a face you upgrade in round eight barely matters.

walkthrough.title: Balance Dice Upgrades with Heroic Feats
walkthrough.text: It is tempting to spend the whole game upgrading your dice, but Heroic Feat cards are where most Glory Points come from. The best strategy is usually to invest heavily in dice during the first three or four rounds, then pivot toward spending your improved resource income on cards. If you only buy die faces, you will have amazing dice but few Glory Points. If you only buy cards, you will struggle to afford the expensive ones.

walkthrough.title: Do Not Ignore Sun and Moon Shards
walkthrough.text: New players often focus entirely on Gold because it is the most intuitive resource. But Sun and Moon Shards are essential for buying Heroic Feat cards, and Sun Shards also let you take extra actions. Forging a Sun Shard face onto one of your dice early gives you a steady income that opens up powerful card purchases and extra turns throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Everyone Rolls Every Turn
walkthrough.text: This is the rule that new players most often forget, and it changes everything about how the game feels. You are never sitting idle waiting for your turn. Pay attention during other players' Divine Blessings because your dice are producing resources too. It also means the game moves faster than you might expect, so plan your turn while others are acting.

walkthrough.title: Ousting Can Be a Gift
walkthrough.text: Sending another player back to the starting portal sounds mean, but remember that the ousted player gets a Minor Blessing. Sometimes ousting an opponent actually helps them by giving them a free resource roll. Do not avoid ousting if you need a specific card, but do be aware that you are giving your opponent something in return. Conversely, if someone ousts you, enjoy the free roll.

walkthrough.title: Replace Your Weakest Faces First
walkthrough.text: When you forge a new face onto your die, always replace the least valuable face currently on that die. There is no reason to replace a two-Gold face when you still have one-Gold faces sitting there. Look at all six faces on each die before deciding which one to swap out. This seems obvious, but in the excitement of getting a new face, players sometimes forget to check.

walkthrough.title: Watch Your Resource Caps
walkthrough.text: Gold caps at twelve, Sun Shards at six, Moon Shards at six. Any resources gained beyond the cap are lost forever. If your Gold is sitting at eleven and you roll a three-Gold face, you only gain one. Spend resources before they overflow. If your Sun Shards are nearly maxed, it might be time to buy a card or take an extra action rather than let shards go to waste.

summary.title: Upgrade Your Gold Faces Early
bullets: Early die upgrades compound over all remaining rounds
A round-1 upgrade pays off 30+ times; a round-8 upgrade barely matters

summary.title: Balance Dice Upgrades with Heroic Feats
bullets: Invest in dice early (rounds 1-4), pivot to cards later
Cards are the main source of Glory Points

summary.title: Do Not Ignore Sun and Moon Shards
bullets: Needed to buy Heroic Feat cards
Sun Shards also fund extra actions (2 per extra turn)

summary.title: Everyone Rolls Every Turn
bullets: ALL players roll during every Divine Blessing
Plan your turn while others are acting

summary.title: Ousting Can Be a Gift
bullets: Ousted player gets a Minor Blessing (free roll)
Do not avoid ousting if you need the card, but know the tradeoff

summary.title: Replace Your Weakest Faces First
bullets: Always swap out the lowest-value face on the die
Check all 6 faces before choosing which to replace

summary.title: Watch Your Resource Caps
bullets: Gold: 12, Sun Shards: 6, Moon Shards: 6
Excess is lost — spend before you overflow

walkthrough.title: The Gold Engine Rush
walkthrough.text: The most reliable opening strategy is to rush Gold die face upgrades in the first two to three rounds. Replace all your one-Gold faces with three-Gold or four-Gold faces as quickly as possible. This creates a Gold engine that generates massive income every Divine Blessing, which then funds everything else. The key insight is that every Gold face you upgrade early gets rolled dozens of times across the full game, so the cumulative return is enormous.

walkthrough.title: Shard-Heavy Opening
walkthrough.text: An alternative to the Gold rush is to forge Sun and Moon Shard faces early. This sacrifices short-term Gold income for rapid access to Heroic Feat cards and extra actions. The advantage is that you can grab the best cards before opponents can afford them, and some cards have reinforcement effects that generate resources every turn, effectively replacing what your dice would have produced. This is higher risk but can be devastating if the card pool has strong reinforcement options.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Temple Supply
walkthrough.text: Pay attention to which die faces are running low in the Temple. If a powerful face is down to one or two copies, you need to decide now whether to grab it before it is gone. Similarly, if a popular face is already sold out, you need to pivot your strategy. Watch what faces your opponents are buying to predict what they are going for. If everyone is buying Gold faces, the shard strategy becomes stronger because those cards will be uncontested.

walkthrough.title: Timing the Extra Action
walkthrough.text: The two-Sun-Shard extra action is one of the most powerful plays in the game, but timing it correctly is crucial. The ideal turns for extra actions are when you can both forge a key die face AND pick up a high-value card, getting two major plays in a single turn. Hoarding Sun Shards just for extra actions is often better than spending them on mediocre cards. A well-timed double turn can swing the game.

walkthrough.title: Reinforcement Card Synergies
walkthrough.text: Reinforcement cards activate every turn during your Call for Reinforcements step, making them the most valuable ongoing effects in the game. Look for combinations where one reinforcement feeds another. For example, a card that grants a Minor Blessing every turn generates resources that fuel other card costs. Stacking multiple reinforcements creates a snowball effect where each turn generates more value than the last.

walkthrough.title: Late-Game Glory Point Faces
walkthrough.text: Some die faces in the Temple grant Glory Points directly when rolled. These are generally weak in the early game because they do not generate spendable resources, but in the last two or three rounds they can be extremely efficient. If you have already built your engine and have leftover Gold, forging a Glory Point face in round eight or nine converts your remaining rolls directly into victory points without needing to buy cards.

walkthrough.title: Deny Key Cards Through Ousting
walkthrough.text: Ousting is not just about reaching the portal you want. It is also about denying opponents access to cards they need. If an opponent is building toward a specific strategy that requires a card at a particular portal, you can move to that portal to oust them and force them to spend another turn getting back. Even if you do not need the card yourself, the tempo loss for your opponent can be worth it. Just remember they get a Minor Blessing, so it is not free disruption.

walkthrough.title: Managing the Resource Cap
walkthrough.text: Advanced players think about resource caps several turns ahead. If your dice are generating six Gold per roll but you only need four Gold for your next purchase, you are wasting two Gold every roll. Either buy more expensive faces that consume the excess, or shift to shard faces so your income is more diverse. The worst outcome in Dice Forge is rolling big numbers that go to waste because you are already capped.

walkthrough.title: Two-Player Adjustments
walkthrough.text: In a two-player game, each player rolls twice for the Divine Blessing at the start of each turn. This means dice upgrades are even more valuable because they trigger twice as often per turn. The Gold rush strategy becomes even stronger, and resource caps are reached faster. Adjust by planning higher-cost purchases and spending more aggressively to avoid wasting capped resources.

walkthrough.title: The Final Round Calculation
walkthrough.text: In the last round, your priorities shift completely. There is no point in upgrading dice or building engine, because there are no more future rolls to benefit from it. Spend every remaining resource on Glory Points. Buy the highest-GP cards you can afford, forge Glory Point faces for your last rolls if possible, and make sure your extra action goes toward points, not engine. The player who transitions from building to scoring at the right moment usually wins.

summary.title: The Gold Engine Rush
bullets: Replace 1-Gold faces with 3- or 4-Gold faces in rounds 1-3
Compound returns: early upgrades get rolled dozens of times

summary.title: Shard-Heavy Opening
bullets: Forge Sun/Moon faces early for fast card access
Higher risk, but grabs best cards before opponents can afford them

summary.title: Reading the Temple Supply
bullets: Watch which faces are running low — grab them before they're gone
If opponents all buy Gold faces, shard strategy becomes uncontested

summary.title: Timing the Extra Action
bullets: Best when you can forge a face AND grab a card in one turn
Hoarding Sun Shards for the right moment is often optimal

summary.title: Reinforcement Card Synergies
bullets: Reinforcements activate every turn — highest ongoing value
Stack multiple for snowball effect

summary.title: Late-Game Glory Point Faces
bullets: GP die faces are weak early but efficient in rounds 8-10
Convert leftover Gold into direct victory points

summary.title: Deny Key Cards Through Ousting
bullets: Oust opponents from portals with cards they need
Forces them to spend a turn returning — tempo advantage

summary.title: Managing the Resource Cap
bullets: Plan purchases to avoid wasting capped resources
Diversify income if one resource overflows consistently

summary.title: Two-Player Adjustments
bullets: Double Divine Blessing makes dice upgrades even more valuable
Resource caps hit faster — spend aggressively

summary.title: The Final Round Calculation
bullets: Last round: stop building, start scoring
Spend every remaining resource on Glory Points

entries.definition: The primary currency used to buy die faces from the Sanctuary section of the Temple. Tracked on the Hero Inventory from 0 to 12. Gained mainly from die rolls during Divine Blessings.
A resource used to buy Heroic Feat cards and to pay for the extra action (2 Sun Shards). Tracked from 0 to 6 on the Hero Inventory. Gained from die faces and some card effects.
A resource used to buy certain Heroic Feat cards from the Islands board. Tracked from 0 to 6 on the Hero Inventory. Gained from die faces and some card effects.
Victory points tracked from 0 to 99 on the Hero Inventory. When passing 99, take a 100 GP token and reset to 0. Final score = track value + 100 GP tokens + card Glory Points. Highest total wins.
Die faces that produce Gold when rolled. Range from 1 Gold (starting) to higher values. Purchased from the Sanctuary for Gold. Upgrading Gold faces early creates a compounding income engine.
Die faces that produce Sun Shards when rolled. Essential for funding Heroic Feats and extra actions. Purchased from the Sanctuary or Gardens depending on the specific face.
Die faces that produce Moon Shards when rolled. Needed for certain Heroic Feat cards. Available in the Temple pools.
Die faces that grant Glory Points directly when rolled. Most effective in the late game when building your engine is no longer useful. Convert future rolls into direct victory points.
Some die faces produce a combination of resources when rolled, such as Gold plus a Sun Shard, or a choice between two options. These provide flexibility and efficiency.

entries.term: Make an Offering to the Gods
entries.definition: One of the two main actions. Buy one or more die faces from the Temple by paying their cost in Gold (Sanctuary) or Sun/Moon Shards (Gardens). Forge each purchased face onto a die immediately. Cannot buy two copies of the same face in one Offering.
One of the two main actions. Pay the Sun/Moon Shard cost of a Heroic Feat card, move your pawn to that portal on the Islands board, and take the top card. Resolve any instant effects immediately.
After your main action, you may spend 2 Sun Shards to take one additional action (either Make an Offering or Perform a Heroic Feat). Limited to once per turn. Powerful for double-play turns.
Step 2 of each turn. The active player activates reinforcement effects on previously collected Heroic Feat cards. Each reinforcement can be used once per turn, in any order.
Heroic Feat cards with effects that trigger once, immediately when the card is taken. The effect resolves right away and does not repeat. Many instant cards also provide Glory Points at game end.
Heroic Feat cards with effects that can be activated once per turn during the Call for Reinforcements step. These provide ongoing value every turn and are among the most powerful cards in the game.
Heroic Feat cards with effects that are always active without needing activation. These provide continuous benefits as long as you hold the card.

Players organize their collected Heroic Feat cards into three piles by permanent effect type: instant-only, reinforcement, and passive. This makes it easy to see which reinforcements to activate each turn.
Each player's personal board with four resource tracks: Gold (0-12), Sun Shards (0-6), Moon Shards (0-6), and Glory Points (0-99). Markers slide along the tracks as resources are gained and spent.
Each player has two dice with removable faces. The light die and dark die start with basic faces and are upgraded throughout the game by forging new faces from the Temple.
The central game board showing portals connected to Heroic Feat card stacks. Players move their hero pawns between portals to perform Heroic Feats. Also contains the round track.
The vertical display board holding all purchasable die faces. The Sanctuary has 10 pools of faces costing Gold. The Gardens has 5 pools of faces costing Sun or Moon Shards.
Spaces on the Islands board where hero pawns stand. Each portal is associated with a stack of Heroic Feat cards. Moving to an occupied portal ousts the other player.
A token that advances one space per round on the Islands board track. Indicates the current round and signals game end when it reaches the final space (round 9 or 10 depending on player count).
Tokens taken when a player's Glory Point marker passes 99 on the Hero Inventory. The marker resets to 0 and the token is kept. Each token is worth 100 Glory Points at game end.
Step 1 of every turn. ALL players roll both of their dice and gain the resources shown. In a 2-player game, each player rolls twice. This is the primary way resources enter the game.
Roll only one die of your choice and gain its resources. Triggered by certain card effects and when a player is ousted. Less powerful than a Divine Blessing but still valuable.

The act of replacing a die face. Use the new face as a lever to pry off the old face, then snap the new face into the empty slot. The old face is kept nearby. Must be done immediately when a face is purchased.
When a player moves their pawn to an already-occupied portal, the previous occupant is sent back to the starting portal and receives a Minor Blessing as compensation.
Maximum values for each resource: Gold 12, Sun Shards 6, Moon Shards 6, Glory Points 99 (then token). Any resources gained beyond the cap are lost. Manage spending to avoid waste.
The portal where all players begin and where ousted players are sent. It is the default position on the Islands board.
